#include <string>

#include "IometerTypes.h"
6
7
// Platform-neutral access-specification helpers shared by both GUIs:
8
//   - naming/formatting (was Qt formatSizeCompact / accessSpecDisplayLabel and
9
//     MFC AccessSpecList::SmartName),
10
//   - the built-in/default access specifications (was Qt builtinAccessSpecs() and
11
//     MFC AccessSpecList::InsertDefaultSpecs).
12
// Operate on the friendly core model (AccessSpec / AccessSpecLine); no GUI types.
13
14
namespace iocore {
15
16
// Format a size in bytes as the largest unit that fits: "X MiB" / "Y KiB" / "Z B".
17
std::string formatSize(int bytes);
18
19
// Display label for an access spec. A single-line spec renders as
20
// "64 KiB; 100% Read; 0% random"; a multi-line (or empty) spec renders as its name.
21
std::string accessSpecLabel(const AccessSpec &spec);
22
23
// The "smart name" text for a single I/O pattern (MFC AccessSpecList::SmartName's
24
// formatting): "<size> <random part> <read part>", e.g. "4 KiB random reads",
25
// "512 B sequential 50% reads", "8 KiB 30% random writes". Size rule: 512 ->
26
// "512 B", 1 MiB -> "1 MiB", else "N KiB". Uniqueness suffixes are the caller's
27
// concern (they need the spec list).
28
std::string smartNameText(int sizeBytes, int randomPct, int readPct);
29
30
// The built-in access specifications: Idle, Default (64 KiB seq read), the
31
// size x read-mix matrix, and the combined "All in one" spec.
32
std::vector<AccessSpec> defaultAccessSpecs();
33
34
// Fill one wire access-spec row from the friendly AccessSpecLine model. Templated
35
// on the wire struct so it works for ANY type with the canonical fields
36
// (of_size/reads/random/delay/burst/reply/size/align) - the canonical Access_Spec
37
// (IOAccess.h) or a GUI's byte-compatible equivalent (e.g. Qt's DyAccessSpec).
38
// The friendly->wire semantics live here so both GUIs share one mapping:
39
//   random  = 100 - seqPercent
40
//   size    = sizeBytes (0 -> 65536 default)
41
//   align   = sizeBytes-if-(-1) / 512-if-0 / explicit-if->0
42
template <typename WireAccess>
43
void fillWireAccess(WireAccess &a, const AccessSpecLine &line)
44
3
{
45
3
    a.of_size = line.ofSize;
46
3
    a.reads   = line.readPercent;
47
3
    a.random  = 100 - line.seqPercent;
48
3
    a.delay   = static_cast<decltype(a.delay)>(line.delayMs);
49
3
    a.burst   = line.burstLength;
50
3
    a.reply   = static_cast<decltype(a.reply)>(line.replyBytes);
51
3
    a.size    = static_cast<decltype(a.size)>(line.sizeBytes > 0 ? line.sizeBytes : 65536);
52
3
    if      (line.alignBytes < 0)  a.align = a.size;                                  // request-size
53
2
    else if (line.alignBytes == 0) a.align = static_cast<decltype(a.align)>(512);      // sector
54
1
    else                           a.align = static_cast<decltype(a.align)>(line.alignBytes);
55
3
}

#pragma once
2
3
#include <string>
4
#include <vector>
5
#include "IometerTypes.h"
6
7
// Platform-neutral global access-specification list management, shared by both
8
// GUIs. Ported from the MFC AccessSpecList (the canonical spec - its semantics
9
// take precedence): "Untitled n" naming for new specs, "Copy of X (n)" unique
10
// naming for copies, case-insensitive name lookup, the default new-spec line
11
// (2 KiB, 67% read, 100% random), and SmartName ("4 KiB random reads" style).
12
//
13
// Index-based (the friendly AccessSpec model has value semantics). Worker
14
// references to deleted specs are the front-end's concern (MFC clears them via
15
// its ManagerList; Qt via its engine) - removeAt only edits the list.
16
17
namespace iocore {
18
19
class AccessSpecLibrary {
20
public:
21
    // The MFC default new-spec line (AccessSpecList::InitAccessSpecLine):
22
    // 2048 B, 100% of size, 67% read, 100% random, burst 1, align = request size.
23
    static AccessSpecLine defaultLine();
24
25
    // Starts empty. loadDefaults() seeds Idle + the built-in catalog specs
26
    // (what the MFC constructor does via InsertIdleSpec/InsertDefaultSpecs).
27
15
    AccessSpecLibrary() = default;
28
    void loadDefaults();
29
30
    // Wholesale replace/read (e.g. ICF load, or syncing with a GUI's list).
31
3
    void setSpecs(std::vector<AccessSpec> specs) { m_specs = std::move(specs); }
32
3
    const std::vector<AccessSpec> &specs() const { return m_specs; }
33
34
187
    int count() const { return static_cast<int>(m_specs.size()); }
35
32
    AccessSpec       &at(int index)       { return m_specs[index]; }
36
0
    const AccessSpec &at(int index) const { return m_specs[index]; }
37
38
    // Create a new spec ("Untitled n", default line, not default-assigned).
39
    // Returns its index.
40
    int newSpec();
41
42
    // Duplicate the spec at `index` as "Copy of <name> (n)" (n = first unique).
43
    // Returns the new spec's index, or -1 if `index` is invalid.
44
    int copySpec(int index);
45
46
    // Remove the spec at `index`. Returns false if out of range.
47
    bool removeAt(int index);
48
49
    // Case-insensitive name lookup (MFC RefByName). -1 if not found.
50
    int indexByName(const std::string &name) const;
51
52
    // The next free "Untitled n" name (MFC NextUntitled).
53
    std::string nextUntitledName() const;
54
55
    // Assign the spec a descriptive name from its single line (MFC SmartName):
56
    // "<size> <random part> <read part>", e.g. "4 KiB random reads",
57
    // "512 B sequential 50% reads", unique-suffixed with " 2", " 3", ... .
58
    // Multi-line specs keep their existing name.
59
    void smartName(int index);
60
61
private:
62
    std::vector<AccessSpec> m_specs;
63
};
64
65
} // namespace iocore

#pragma once
2
3
#include <string>
4
#include <cstdint>
5
#include <functional>
6
7
// Forward declarations
8
struct DyMsg;
9
struct DyDataMessage;
10
11
// Encapsulates TCP connection and protocol handshake (platform-agnostic)
12
// Handles:
13
//   - TCP socket on port 1066
14
//   - DY_LOGIN message exchange
15
//   - Manager_Info data reception
16
//   - Message framing
17
// Used by the GUI front-ends (each wraps it with its own socket transport)
18
19
class DynamoConnection {
20
public:
21
    enum class State {
22
        Disconnected,
23
        Connecting,
24
        WaitingLoginData,
25
        Connected,
26
        Error
27
    };
28
29
    DynamoConnection();
30
    ~DynamoConnection();
31
32
    // Initiate login handshake with Dynamo
33
    bool startLogin(const std::string &managerName, int timeoutMs = 30000);
34
35
    // Send raw 8-byte message (e.g., DY_LOGIN, START, STOP, RECORD_ON/OFF)
36
    bool sendMessage(const DyMsg &msg);
37
38
    // Receive data message (Manager_Info, Target data, Worker results, etc.)
39
    // Returns nullptr if timeout or error
40
    DyDataMessage* receiveDataMessage(int timeoutMs = 5000);
41
42
    // Check if connection is active
43
5
    State state() const { return m_state; }
44
3
    bool isConnected() const { return m_state == State::Connected; }
45
46
    // Get connection details
47
    std::string remoteAddress() const;
48
    int remotePort() const;
49
50
    // Close connection gracefully
51
    void disconnect();
52
53
    // Optional callbacks (for GUI integration)
54
    std::function<void(const std::string&)> onConnected;
55
    std::function<void()> onDisconnected;
56
    std::function<void(DyDataMessage*)> onDataReceived;
57
    std::function<void(const std::string&)> onError;
58
59
private:
60
    bool waitForBytes(int count, int timeoutMs);
61
    bool receiveExactly(int count);
62
63
    void *m_socket;  // Opaque pointer to the GUI's platform-specific socket object
64
    State m_state;
65
    std::string m_managerName;
66
    std::string m_buffer;  // Input buffer for partial messages
67
68
    static constexpr int DYNAMO_PORT = 1066;
69
    static constexpr int MESSAGE_SIZE = 8;
70
};

#pragma once
2
3
#include <string>
4
#include <vector>
5
#include <cstdint>
6
7
// Section loaders for ICF configuration files - faithful ports of the MFC
8
// section parsers (the canonical behavior; see docs/dev/ICF_PARSER_UNIFICATION_PLAN.md).
9
// Phase 2 covers 'TEST SETUP (CPageSetup::LoadConfig) and 'RESULTS DISPLAY
10
// (CPageDisplay::LoadConfig). Each loader opens its own stream and seeks its
11
// own section, exactly like the MFC code, so loaders are independent and a
12
// front-end can restore sections selectively.
13
//
14
// Loader semantics (shared):
15
//  - missing section        -> true (nothing to restore; "this is OK")
16
//  - bad version line       -> false
17
//  - parse errors           -> false, with the EXACT MFC dialog texts in
18
//                              errors() (in dialog order; some "Ignoring..."
19
//                              errors record text and continue, like MFC)
20
//  - loaders OVERWRITE ONLY the fields whose keys are present in the file
21
//    (MFC writes directly into page members, retaining prior values for
22
//    missing keys) - callers pre-fill structs with current state.
23
24
namespace iocore {
25
26
// 'TEST SETUP - numeric values mirror the MFC enums (raw integers can flow in
27
// from legacy files, so the encodings must match):
28
//   resultType:  RecordAll=0, NoTargets=1, NoWorkers=2, NoManagers=3, None=4
29
//   stepType:    StepLinear=0, StepExponential=1
30
//   testType:    NORMAL=0, CyclingTargets=1, CyclingWorkers=2,
31
//                IncTargetsParallel=3, IncTargetsSerial=4, WorkersTargets=5,
32
//                CyclingQueue=6, CyclingQueueTargets=7
33
//   workerCount: -1 = NUMBER_OF_CPUS
34
struct IcfTestSetup {
35
    std::string testName;
36
    int hours = 0, minutes = 0, seconds = 0;
37
    int rampTime = 0;
38
    int diskWorkerCount = 0;
39
    int netWorkerCount  = 0;
40
    int resultType = 0;
41
    struct Cycling { int start = 1, end = 1, step = 1, stepType = 0; };
42
    Cycling workerCycling, targetCycling, queueCycling;   // queue uses end too
43
    int testType = 0;
44
};
45
46
// 'RESULTS DISPLAY - whichPerf: WHOLE_TEST_PERF=0, LAST_UPDATE_PERF=1.
47
struct IcfDisplaySettings {
48
    struct Bar {
49
        enum Kind { Statistic, Manager, Worker };
50
        int         index = 0;     // zero-based (file is one-based, 1..6)
51
        Kind        kind  = Statistic;
52
        std::string name;          // statistic name / manager name / worker name
53
        int         id    = 0;     // manager/worker ID (unused for Statistic)
54
    };
55
    bool updateLinePresent = false;   // the Record/Frequency/Type key was seen
56
    bool recordLastUpdate  = false;
57
    int  updateFrequency   = 0;
58
    int  whichPerf         = 0;
59
    std::vector<Bar> bars;            // in file order; apply sequentially
60
};
61
62
// 'ACCESS SPECIFICATIONS - raw wire-format numbers, exactly as in the file.
63
// defaultAssignment mirrors the MFC enum: AssignNone=0, AssignAll=1,
64
// AssignDisk=2, AssignNet=3 (raw integers can flow in from legacy files).
65
struct IcfAccessSpecLine {
66
    int size = 0, ofSize = 0, reads = 0, random = 0;
67
    int delay = 0, burst = 0, align = 0, reply = 0;
68
};
69
struct IcfAccessSpec {
70
    std::string name;
71
    int defaultAssignment = 0;
72
    std::vector<IcfAccessSpecLine> lines;
73
};
74
75
// 'MANAGER LIST - the per-manager / per-worker configuration, as raw parsed data.
76
// The MFC live-object side-effects (loadmap manager lookup, access-spec name
77
// validation, target matching against discovered disks / remote-manager NICs)
78
// are the front-end adapter's job; the loader returns pure data only.
79
// Invalid mirrors MFC's InvalidType: it is what a worker keeps when its type
80
// token is an unknown NETWORK subtype (MFC records the error but does NOT abort,
81
// leaving wkr_type_hex unchanged - InvalidType for the first such worker).
82
enum class IcfWorkerKind { Invalid = -1, Disk = 0, NetTCP = 1, NetVI = 2 };
83
struct IcfTargetConfig {
84
    std::string    name;
85
    IcfWorkerKind  kind = IcfWorkerKind::Disk;   // DISK / NETWORK,TCP / NETWORK,VI
86
    // Network targets only: the remote manager that owns the target NIC.
87
    int            targetManagerId = 0;
88
    std::string    targetManagerName;
89
};
90
struct IcfWorkerConfig {
91
    std::string    name;
92
    IcfWorkerKind  kind = IcfWorkerKind::Disk;
93
    // 'Default target settings for worker (all optional; MFC defaults shown):
94
    int            queueDepth     = 1;
95
    bool           testConnRate   = false;
96
    int            transPerConn   = 1;
97
    bool           useFixedSeed   = false;
98
    uint64_t       fixedSeedValue = 0;
99
    uint64_t       diskMaxSize    = 0;
100
    uint64_t       startingSector = 0;
101
    int            dataPattern    = 0;
102
    std::string    localNetworkInterface;
103
    int            viOutstandingIos = 0;
104
    bool           hasDefaultSettings = false;   // a settings block was present
105
    // Which 'Default target settings lines actually appeared. MFC writes a
106
    // setting ONLY when its key is present; an absent key keeps the worker's
107
    // prior value, so a faithful adapter must apply selectively.
108
    bool           ioSettingsPresent    = false; // 'Number of outstanding IOs... line
109
    bool           fixedSeedPresent     = false; // ...the use-fixed-seed variant
110
    bool           diskSizePresent      = false; // 'Disk maximum size,starting sector line
111
    bool           dataPatternPresent   = false; // ...the Data pattern variant
112
    bool           localNetIfacePresent = false; // 'Local network interface
113
    bool           viIosPresent         = false; // 'VI outstanding IOs
114
    std::vector<std::string>     assignedSpecNames;
115
    std::vector<IcfTargetConfig> targets;
116
};
117
struct IcfManagerConfig {
118
    std::string                  name;
119
    int                          id = 1;
120
    std::string                  address;
121
    std::vector<IcfWorkerConfig> workers;
122
};
123
124
class IcfDocument {
125
public:
126
260
    explicit IcfDocument(const std::string &path) : m_path(path) {}
127
128
    bool loadTestSetup(IcfTestSetup &io);
129
    bool loadResultsDisplay(IcfDisplaySettings &io);
130
131
    // Load the 'MANAGER LIST section (managers, their workers, each worker's
132
    // default settings, assigned spec NAMES and target NAMES+types). A faithful
133
    // port of ManagerList/Manager/Worker::LoadConfig's PARSING; the caller does
134
    // the live matching/validation. loadAspecs/loadTargets false skips (but still
135
    // consumes) those worker sub-sections, like the MFC flags. Network targets
136
    // record their owning manager (targetManagerId/Name); the live NIC matching
137
    // and CreateNetClient remain the front-end adapter's job.
138
    bool loadManagerList(std::vector<IcfManagerConfig> &out,
139
                         bool loadAspecs = true, bool loadTargets = true);
140
141
    // Load the global access specifications, dispatching on the file version
142
    // like AccessSpecList::LoadConfig: >= 1998.05.21 uses the modern named
143
    // format; older files use the raw numeric format (specs are then named by
144
    // the SmartName rules and default-assigned to all workers). New-format
145
    // specs REPLACE an earlier spec of the same name (case-insensitive) within
146
    // `out`. `existingNames` (optional) are names already in the caller's live
147
    // list - they participate in the old format's Untitled/SmartName
148
    // uniqueness checks, like the MFC live-list lookups did.
149
    bool loadAccessSpecs(std::vector<IcfAccessSpec> &out,
150
                         const std::vector<std::string> *existingNames = nullptr);
151
152
    // The file's encoded version (IcfStream::BadVersion on error). Does not
153
    // touch errors(); used by adapters that need format decisions (e.g. the
154
    // old-format default-spec assignment).
155
    uint64_t readVersion() const;
156
157
    // MFC dialog texts from the LAST load call, in the order MFC would have
158
    // shown them (front-end adapters display these verbatim).
159
26
    const std::vector<std::string> &errors() const { return m_errors; }
160
161
private:
162
    std::string              m_path;
163
    std::vector<std::string> m_errors;
164
};
165
166
} // namespace iocore

#pragma once
2
3
#include <fstream>
4
#include <string>
5
#include <vector>
6
#include <cstdint>
7
8
// Platform-neutral port of the MFC ICF_ifstream (ICF_ifstream.h/.cpp) - the
9
// low-level primitives every ICF section loader is built on. The MFC behavior is
10
// CANONICAL (see docs/dev/ICF_PARSER_UNIFICATION_PLAN.md): line length limit, trimming,
11
// case-insensitive prefix matching, the dual version-encoding scheme (including
12
// its dead 3.26.97 special case), and the exact error-message texts (the MFC
13
// code shows them in dialogs; here they are collected via errors()/lastError()
14
// so a front-end adapter can display them verbatim).
15
16
namespace iocore {
17
18
class IcfStream {
19
public:
20
    // MFC MAX_ICF_LINE: lines are read with getline(buf, 200); a longer line
21
    // truncates to 199 chars and puts the stream in a failed state.
22
    static const int MaxLine = 200;
23
24
    // getVersion() error result (the MFC code returns -1 in a DWORDLONG).
25
    static const uint64_t BadVersion = ~0ULL;
26
27
    // Binary mode: byte-exact tellg/seekg (getPair's empty-section push-back).
28
    // MFC opens text-mode, which only seeks correctly on CRLF files - the only
29
    // kind the Windows GUI writes; trimming handles the '\r', so behavior for
30
    // every CRLF file is identical to MFC, and LF-only files work too (in MFC
31
    // text mode those could mis-seek - a glitch, not a behavior to preserve).
32
278
    explicit IcfStream(const std::string &path) : m_file(path, std::ios::binary) {}
33
34
0
    bool isOpen() const { return m_file.is_open(); }
35
0
    bool eof()    const { return m_file.eof(); }
36
    // MFC callers test rdstate() directly ("if (infile.rdstate())").
37
4.35k
    bool failed() const { return m_file.rdstate() != std::ios::goodbit; }
38
4.62k
    int  peek()         { return m_file.peek(); }
39
40
    // Read the version line ("Version 1.1.0" / "Version 1998.10.08") and encode:
41
    //   major > 1900 (date):   major*10000 + middle*100 + minor
42
    //   else       (semver):   major*10000000000 + middle*100000 + minor
43
    // so semver always orders above every date version. The version==32697
44
    // special case (3.26.97 -> 19980105) is preserved verbatim even though the
45
    // current formulas make it unreachable from "3.26.97" itself (Phase-0
46
    // verified). Versions < 19980105 record an error but are still returned.
47
    uint64_t getVersion();
48
49
    // Skip lines until one *starts with* `identifier` (case-insensitive, both
50
    // sides trimmed). Consumes the matched line. False at EOF.
51
    bool skipTo(const std::string &identifier);
52
53
    // Next line, trimmed both ends. Lines >= MaxLine chars truncate and fail the
54
    // stream (MFC getline(buf,200) behavior). Empty string on closed file.
55
    std::string getNextLine();
56
57
    // Read a 'key comment line and its following value line. 'End*/'Version*
58
    // keys have no value. Extra comment lines before the value are skipped -
59
    // unless one is an 'End*/'Version* line (empty section), which is pushed
60
    // back for the next call and an empty value returned (success).
61
    bool getPair(std::string &key, std::string &value);
62
63
    // Raw stream access for the access-spec parsers, which read with
64
    // operator>> and getline-with-delimiter exactly like the MFC code.
65
76
    std::ifstream &raw() { return m_file; }
66
67
    // Collected MFC error texts (ErrorMessage equivalents), oldest first.
68
2
    const std::vector<std::string> &errors() const { return m_errors; }
69
3
    std::string lastError() const { return m_errors.empty() ? std::string() : m_errors.back(); }
70
71
    // ---- static field extractors (port of the ICF_ifstream statics) ---------
72
    // On failure they leave/restore `s` per MFC and write the exact MFC error
73
    // text to *err (when provided).
74
    static bool extractFirstInt(std::string &s, int &n, std::string *err = nullptr);
75
    static bool extractFirstUInt64(std::string &s, uint64_t &n, std::string *err = nullptr);
76
    static bool extractFirstIntVersion(std::string &s, int &n, std::string *err = nullptr);
77
    // First token of ICF "token characters" (no comma/pipe; spaces only when
78
    // requested); consumes it from `s` and trims the remainder.
79
    static std::string extractFirstToken(std::string &s, bool spaces = false);
80
81
private:
82
6
    void addError(const std::string &msg) { m_errors.push_back(msg); }
83
84
    std::ifstream            m_file;
85
    std::vector<std::string> m_errors;
86
};
87
88
} // namespace iocore

// ---- Access specification ---------------------------------------------------
114
115
// One I/O pattern row inside an AccessSpec (mirrors Test_Spec::access[i])
116
struct AccessSpecLine {
117
    int    sizeBytes   = 65536;  // transfer size in bytes
118
    int    ofSize      = 100;    // % of access specification (% Access column)
119
    int    readPercent = 100;    // % reads (0-100)
120
    int    seqPercent  = 100;    // % sequential; randomPercent = 100 - seqPercent
121
    int    burstLength = 1;      // burst length (I/Os)
122
    double delayMs     = 0.0;    // transfer delay in ms
123
    // Alignment: 0 = sector boundaries (512 B on wire),
124
    //           -1 = request-size boundaries (size on wire),
125
    //           >0 = explicit bytes
126
    int    alignBytes  = 0;
127
    int    replyBytes  = 0;      // 0 = no reply (network specs only)
128
};
129
130
struct AccessSpec {
131
    std::string              name        = "Untitled";
132
    bool                     defaultSpec = false;
133
    int                      iterations  = 0;         // 0 = run until stopped
134
    std::vector<AccessSpecLine> lines;                 // I/O pattern rows (≥1 for valid specs)
135
};
136
137
// ---- Worker descriptor ------------------------------------------------------
138
139
struct WorkerInfo {
140
    std::string            id;
141
    std::string            name;
142
    std::string            type;          // "Disk" or "Network"
143
    std::string            managerName;
144
    std::vector<std::string> targets;       // assigned disk/network targets
145
    std::vector<std::string> assignedSpecs; // access spec names assigned (empty = use default)
146
    int                    queueDepth     = 1;
147
    int                    activeSpec     = 0;
148
149
    // Disk Targets tab parameters (match original's right-panel fields):
150
    int64_t     maxDiskSize    = 0;       // sectors; 0 = use entire disk
151
    int64_t     startingSector = 0;
152
    bool        useFixedSeed   = false;
153
    int64_t     fixedSeedValue = 0;
154
    bool        testConnRate   = false;
155
    int         transPerConn   = 1;
156
    int         dataPattern    = 0;       // 0=Repeating bytes, 1=Pseudo-random, 2=Full random
157
158
    // Network Targets tab parameters:
159
    std::string networkInterface = "";   // local NIC to use for outgoing connections
160
    int         maxOutstandingSends = 1; // "Max # Outstanding Sends" (network queue depth)
161
};
162
163
// ---- Manager descriptor -----------------------------------------------------
164
165
struct ManagerInfo {
166
    std::string             name;
167
    std::string             address;
168
    bool                    connected        = false;
169
    int                     processorCount   = 1;  // CPU count reported by Dynamo
170

171
    std::vector<TargetInfo> availableTargets;      // disk targets reported by Dynamo
172
    std::vector<std::string> availableNetInterfaces;// NIC addresses reported by Dynamo
173
};
174
175
// ---- Test Setup tab configuration -------------------------------------------
176
177
struct TestConfig {

    std::string description    = "";
179
    int         runHours       = 0;
180
    int         runMinutes     = 0;
181
    int         runSeconds     = 0;
182
    int         rampSeconds    = 0;
183
    int         recordResults  = 0;   // 0=All, 1=First Run, 2=Last Run
184
    // Cycling options
185
    int         cyclingMode    = 0;   // 0=Normal
186
    int         workerStart    = 1;
187
    int         workerStep     = 1;
188
    int         workerStepping = 0;   // 0=Linear Stepping
189
    int         targetStart    = 1;
190
    int         targetStep     = 1;
191
    int         targetStepping = 0;
192
    int         ioqStart       = 1;

194
    int         ioqPower       = 2;

    int         ioqStepping    = 1;   // 1=Exponential Stepping

};

#pragma once
2
3
#include <string>
4
#include <vector>
5
#include <functional>
6
#include <cstddef>
7
8
// Platform-neutral manager-restore map: the decision logic for mapping the
9
// managers named in an ICF file to the running manager objects when a config is
10
// loaded. Ported verbatim from the MFC ManagerMap (which is the canonical spec);
11
// both GUIs wrap this so they share one restore/spawn/wait implementation.
12
//
13
// This holds ONLY the decisions (match an incoming manager to a saved entry,
14
// decide which unassigned managers are local and should be spawned, decide if we
15
// are still waiting). It performs NO I/O and touches no GUI: the running manager
16
// object is an opaque Handle the front-end owns, spawning is returned as a list
17
// for the front-end to act on, and the waiting-list dialog / local machine name
18
// are the front-end's concern.
19
20
namespace iocore {
21
22
class ManagerMap {
23
public:
24
    // Opaque handle to the front-end's running manager object (e.g. the MFC
25
    // Manager* or a Qt object). nullptr means "unassigned / still waiting".
26
    using Handle = void *;
27
28
    // Tests whether a network address is local to this machine.
29
    using IsLocalFn = std::function<bool(const std::string &address)>;
30
31
    // The special "this is the local machine" manager name (matches the MFC
32
    // LocalHostName define, "(Local)").
33
    static const char *const LocalHostName;
34
35
    struct Entry {
36
        std::string name;
37
        int         id = 0;
38
        std::string address;
39
        Handle      handle = nullptr;   // running manager, or null if waiting
40
    };
41
42
    // How the front-end should launch a local Dynamo for an unassigned entry.
43
    // nameArg empty  -> launch with default local name (no -n);
44
    // nameArg set    -> launch passing " -n <nameArg>".
45
    struct SpawnRequest {
46
        std::string nameArg;
47
    };
48
49
    // Prepare for reuse.
50
    void reset();
51
52
    // Append an entry (handle may be null = waiting on that manager).
53
    void store(const std::string &name, int id, const std::string &address,
54
               Handle handle = nullptr);
55
56
    // First entry matching name+id, OR any LocalHostName entry; null if none.
57
    Handle retrieve(const std::string &name, int id) const;
58
59
    // On login, assign the first matching unassigned entry the given handle.
60
    // Returns true if a matching entry was found and assigned.
61
    bool managerLoggedIn(const std::string &name, const std::string &address,
62
                         Handle handle);
63
64
    // If there is exactly one entry and it is unassigned, assign it; else false.
65
    bool setIfOneManager(Handle handle);
66
67
    // Mark the entry holding this handle as unassigned again (e.g. the manager
68
    // disconnected); the slot returns to the waiting state. Returns true if a
69
    // matching entry was cleared.
70
    bool managerLoggedOut(Handle handle);
71
72
    // True while any entry is still unassigned (waiting for a login).
73
    bool isWaitingList() const;
74
75
    // True if this handle is one of the mapped managers (i.e. needed by the
76
    // config being restored).
77
    bool isManagerNeeded(Handle handle) const;
78
79
    // Decide which unassigned, LOCAL managers to spawn. `localName` is this
80
    // machine's NetBIOS name; `isLocal` tests an address. Mirrors the MFC
81
    // SpawnLocalManagers decision exactly.
82
    std::vector<SpawnRequest> localManagersToSpawn(const IsLocalFn &isLocal,
83
                                                   const std::string &localName) const;
84
85
5
    std::size_t size() const { return m_entries.size(); }
86
34
    const std::vector<Entry> &entries() const { return m_entries; }
87
88
private:
89
    std::vector<Entry> m_entries;
90
};
91
92
} // namespace iocore

// Decode raw worker/target performance counters into displayable rates and
6
// latencies. This is the single shared implementation of the formula that both
7
// GUIs used to carry independently (MFC Worker::UpdateResults and Qt
8
// QtDySession::decodeWorkerResult). MFC behavior is canonical; this is a faithful
9

10
// casts.
11
//
12
// Like iocore::fillWireAccess, decodeRawResult is TEMPLATED on the raw counter
13
// struct so it serves both the canonical MFC Raw_Result (IOCommon.h) and Qt's
14
// DyRawResult (QtDyProto.h) - the field names are identical. The same function
15
// works on a single target's counters or a summed worker/manager set, because
16
// summing then decoding equals decoding then summing for these rates.
17
18
namespace iocore {
19
20
// Binary / decimal megabyte (match IOCommon.h MEGABYTE_BIN / MEGABYTE_DEC).
21

22
constexpr double kMegabyteDec = 1000000.0;
23
24
// Calculated result members, mirroring the MFC Results struct's derived fields.
25
struct ResultRates {
26

27

29

30

31

32

33

34
    unsigned int totalErrors = 0;
35
};
36
37
// Decode one raw counter set. `runTimeSec` is the rate denominator in seconds

#pragma once
2
3
#include <ostream>
4
#include <iomanip>
5
#include <string>
6
#include <cstdint>
7
8
// Canonical Iometer results-CSV schema, shared by both GUIs. MFC is the correct
9
// format (the ~76-column layout users have parsed for 20 years); this is the
10
// single source of truth for it. The header is byte-identical to the string MFC
11
// historically emitted inline in ManagerList::SaveResults.
12
//
13
// (The per-row writer - one templated function that replaces the 5-6 duplicated
14
// column dumps across ManagerList/Manager/Worker SaveResults - follows in a later
15
// increment; the row variants differ only in which blocks are filled vs blank.)
16
17
namespace iocore {
18
19
// Emit the results section header: the "'Results" line followed by the full
20
// column header line (37 result columns + raw counters + CPU/network + the 21
21
// latency-bin columns). Ends each line with '\n'.
22
inline void writeResultsHeader(std::ostream &out)
23
32
{
24
32
    out << "'Results\n";
25
32
    out << "'Target Type,Target Name,Access Specification Name,# Managers,"
26
32
           "# Workers,# Disks,IOps,Read IOps,Write IOps,MiBps (Binary),Read MiBps (Binary),Write MiBps (Binary),"
27
32
           "MBps (Decimal),Read MBps (Decimal),Write MBps (Decimal),Transactions per Second,Connections per Second,"
28
32
           "Average Response Time,Average Read Response Time,"
29
32
           "Average Write Response Time,Average Transaction Time,"
30
32
           "Average Connection Time,Maximum Response Time,"
31
32
           "Maximum Read Response Time,Maximum Write Response Time,"
32
32
           "Maximum Transaction Time,Maximum Connection Time,"
33
32
           "Errors,Read Errors,Write Errors,Bytes Read,Bytes Written,Read I/Os,"
34
32
           "Write I/Os,Connections,Transactions per Connection,"
35
32
           "Total Raw Read Response Time,Total Raw Write Response Time,"
36
32
           "Total Raw Transaction Time,Total Raw Connection Time,"
37
32
           "Maximum Raw Read Response Time,Maximum Raw Write Response Time,"
38
32
           "Maximum Raw Transaction Time,Maximum Raw Connection Time,"
39
32
           "Total Raw Run Time,Starting Sector,Maximum Size,Queue Depth,"
40
32
           "% CPU Utilization,% User Time,% Privileged Time,% DPC Time,"
41
32
           "% Interrupt Time,Processor Speed,Interrupts per Second,"
42
32
           "CPU Effectiveness,Packets/Second,Packet Errors,Segments Retransmitted/Second"
43
32
           ",0 to 50 uS,50 to 100 uS,100 to 200 uS,200 to 500 uS,0.5 to 1 mS,1 to 2 mS,2 to 5 mS,5 to 10 mS,"
44
32
           "10 to 15 mS,15 to 20 mS,20 to 30 mS,30 to 50 mS,50 to 100 mS,100 to 200 mS,200 to 500 mS,0.5 to 1 S,"
45
32
           "1 to 2 s,2 to 4.7 s,4.7 to 5 s,5 to 10 s, >= 10 s"
46
32
           "\n";
47
32
}
48
49
// One canonical results-CSV data row. All four MFC row variants (ALL / MANAGER /
50
// WORKER / TARGET) share this 80-column layout; they differ only in which blocks
51
// are filled vs left blank, captured here by rawBlock / cpuNetBlock / procSpeed:
52
//   - ALL    : rawBlock=false, cpuNetBlock=true,  procSpeedPresent=false
53
//   - MANAGER: rawBlock=true,  cpuNetBlock=true,  procSpeedPresent=true
54
//   - WORKER : rawBlock=true,  cpuNetBlock=false, procSpeedPresent=true
55
// Doubles are written fixed/6 and integers plain, exactly like the MFC output.
56
// managers/workers/disks and sector/size/queue are strings so a variant can leave
57
// a count blank (MANAGER blanks #Managers; WORKER blanks #Managers and #Workers).
58
struct ResultRow {
59
    std::string targetType, targetName, accessSpec;   // [0-2]
60
    std::string managers, workers, disks;             // [3-5]
61
62
    double iops = 0, readIops = 0, writeIops = 0;                       // [6-8]
63
    double mbpsBin = 0, readMbpsBin = 0, writeMbpsBin = 0;              // [9-11]
64
    double mbpsDec = 0, readMbpsDec = 0, writeMbpsDec = 0;              // [12-14]
65
    double transPerSec = 0, connPerSec = 0;                            // [15-16]
66
    double aveLatency = 0, aveRead = 0, aveWrite = 0, aveTrans = 0, aveConn = 0;   // [17-21]
67
    double maxLatency = 0, maxRead = 0, maxWrite = 0, maxTrans = 0, maxConn = 0;   // [22-26]
68
69
    unsigned long totalErrors = 0, readErrors = 0, writeErrors = 0;    // [27-29]
70
    uint64_t bytesRead = 0, bytesWritten = 0,
71
             readCount = 0, writeCount = 0, connectionCount = 0;       // [30-34]
72
    long long transPerConn = 0;                                        // [35]
73
74
    bool rawBlock = false;                                             // [36-44]
75
    uint64_t rawReadLatSum = 0, rawWriteLatSum = 0,
76
             rawTransLatSum = 0, rawConnLatSum = 0;
77
    uint64_t rawMaxRead = 0, rawMaxWrite = 0, rawMaxTrans = 0, rawMaxConn = 0;
78
    long long rawCounterTime = 0;   // Total Raw Run Time [44]
79
80
    std::string startingSector, maxSize, queueDepth;                  // [45-47]
81
82
    bool cpuNetBlock = false;       // [48-52] CPU + [54] interrupts + [55] eff + [56-58] net
83
    double cpuUtil[5] = {0, 0, 0, 0, 0};

148
// Copy the measured / raw / CPU / network / latency-bin fields from a canonical
149
// MFC Results struct into a ResultRow (the [6-44], [48-58], [59-79] columns).
150
// Templated so core stays free of the MFC Results type (the decodeRawResult
151
// pattern); only MFC instantiates it. The caller sets the metadata columns
152
// ([0-5]), transPerConn, sector/size/queue, and the rawBlock / cpuNetBlock /
153
// procSpeed variant fields. Array indices match IOCommon.h
154
// (CPU_UTILIZATION_RESULTS=5, CPU_IRQ=5, NI_PACKETS=0, NI_ERRORS=1,
155
// TCP_SEGMENTS_RESENT=0, LATENCY_BIN_SIZE=21).
156
template <class ResultsT>
157
void fillResultRow(ResultRow &row, const ResultsT &res)
158
{
159
    row.iops = res.IOps; row.readIops = res.read_IOps; row.writeIops = res.write_IOps;
160
    row.mbpsBin = res.MBps_Bin; row.readMbpsBin = res.read_MBps_Bin; row.writeMbpsBin = res.write_MBps_Bin;
161
    row.mbpsDec = res.MBps_Dec; row.readMbpsDec = res.read_MBps_Dec; row.writeMbpsDec = res.write_MBps_Dec;
162
    row.transPerSec = res.transactions_per_second; row.connPerSec = res.connections_per_second;
163
    row.aveLatency = res.ave_latency; row.aveRead = res.ave_read_latency; row.aveWrite = res.ave_write_latency;
164
    row.aveTrans = res.ave_transaction_latency; row.aveConn = res.ave_connection_latency;
165
    row.maxLatency = res.max_latency; row.maxRead = res.max_read_latency; row.maxWrite = res.max_write_latency;
166
    row.maxTrans = res.max_transaction_latency; row.maxConn = res.max_connection_latency;
167
168
    row.totalErrors = res.total_errors; row.readErrors = res.raw.read_errors; row.writeErrors = res.raw.write_errors;
169
    row.bytesRead = res.raw.bytes_read; row.bytesWritten = res.raw.bytes_written;
170
    row.readCount = res.raw.read_count; row.writeCount = res.raw.write_count;
171
    row.connectionCount = res.raw.connection_count;
172
173
    row.rawReadLatSum = res.raw.read_latency_sum; row.rawWriteLatSum = res.raw.write_latency_sum;
174
    row.rawTransLatSum = res.raw.transaction_latency_sum; row.rawConnLatSum = res.raw.connection_latency_sum;
175
    row.rawMaxRead = res.raw.max_raw_read_latency; row.rawMaxWrite = res.raw.max_raw_write_latency;
176
    row.rawMaxTrans = res.raw.max_raw_transaction_latency; row.rawMaxConn = res.raw.max_raw_connection_latency;
177
    row.rawCounterTime = res.raw.counter_time;
178
179
    for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) row.cpuUtil[i] = res.CPU_utilization[i];
180
    row.interrupts = res.CPU_utilization[5];          // CPU_IRQ
181
    row.effectiveness = res.CPU_effectiveness;
182
    row.niPackets = res.ni_statistics[0]; row.niErrors = res.ni_statistics[1];
183
    row.tcpRetrans = res.tcp_statistics[0];
184
    for (int i = 0; i < 21; ++i) row.latencyBin[i] = res.raw.latency_bin[i];
185
}
186
187
} // namespace iocore

#pragma once
2
3
#include <string>
4
#include <vector>
5
#include <functional>
6
#include <cstdint>
7
#include "IometerTypes.h"
8
9
// Forward declarations (included via IometerTypes.h above, repeated here for clarity)
10
// struct TestConfig;
11
// struct AccessSpec;
12
// struct WorkerInfo;
13
// struct WorkerResult;
14
15
// Platform-agnostic test runner managing test lifecycle
16
// Handles:
17
//   - Test start/stop/stopAll state machine
18
//   - Worker setup and configuration
19
//   - Result collection
20
//   - Progress tracking
21
// Used by both the GUI front-ends
22
23
class TestRunner {
24
25
public:
26
    enum class State {
27
        Idle,           // Ready to start test
28
        Starting,       // Setting up workers
29
        Running,        // Test executing
30
        Stopping,       // Shutting down gracefully
31
        Stopped,        // Test complete, results ready
32
        Error           // Error occurred
33
    };
34
35
    TestRunner();
36
    ~TestRunner();
37
38
    // Test control
39
    bool startTest(const TestConfig &cfg,
40
                   const std::vector<WorkerInfo> &workers,
41
                   const std::vector<AccessSpec> &specs);
42
    bool stopTest();
43
    bool stopAll();
44
45
    // State queries
46
9
    State state() const { return m_state; }
47

// QtBigMeterWidget.cpp
2
// Qt port of CBigMeter - Iometer "Presentation Meter" window.
3
//
4
// Layout (top to bottom) matches the original:
5
//   1. Speedometer gauge - fills most of the window
6
//   2. Large numeric value with units  (blue, ~22pt bold)
7
//   3. Subtitle "All Managers - <metric name>"  (plain, smaller)
8
//   4. Separator
9
//   5. Two side-by-side groups: [Settings] | [Test Controls]
10
11

21
#include <QGroupBox>
22
#include <QFont>
23
#include <QSizePolicy>
24
#include <QFrame>
25
#include <QPalette>
26
27
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
28
// Construction
29
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
30
31

    : QWidget(parent)

{
34
30
    setupUi();

    setWindowTitle("Iometer - Presentation Meter");

    resize(600, 580);

30
}

39

// QtBigMeterWidget.h
2
// Qt port of CBigMeter - the Iometer "Presentation Meter" window.
3
//
4
// Displays a large speedometer (QtMeterWidget) together with:
5
//   • test title and worker/result labels
6
//   • large numeric readout
7
//   • result-type selector (combo box)
8
//   • max-range spin box (0 = auto)
9
//   • watermark check box
10
//   • Start / Next>>  and  Stop  buttons
11
//
12
// Signals mirror the CBigMeter callbacks so the rest of the Qt GUI can
13
// attach the same logic as the MFC version.
14

15
16

17

19

// ---- Simulation tick --------------------------------------------------------
92
93
WorkerResult QtDemoEngine::makeResult(const WorkerInfo &w, const std::string &mgrName, double t) const
94
12
{
95
12
    int idx = 0;
96
30
    for (int i = 0; i < m_managers.first().workers.size(); ++i)
97
30
        if (m_managers.first().workers[i].id == w.id) { idx = i; break; }
98
12
    const double phase = idx * 0.7;
99
100
12
    const double baseMbps = 500.0 + 100.0 * idx;
101
12
    const double waveMbps = 80.0  * std::sin(t * 0.4 + phase);
102
12
    const double spike    = (std::fmod(t + phase, 15.0) < 0.6) ? 250.0 : 0.0;
103
12
    const double mbps     = std::max(10.0, baseMbps + waveMbps + spike);
104
105
12
    const double iops       = (mbps * 1e6) / (64.0 * 1024.0);
106
12
    const double latBase    = 1000.0 / iops * w.queueDepth;
107
12
    const double latJitter  = latBase * 0.15 * std::sin(t * 1.3 + phase);
108
109
12
    WorkerResult r;
110
12
    r.managerName  = mgrName;          // std::string
111
12
    r.workerName   = w.name;           // std::string
112
12
    r.mbpsDec      = mbps;
113
12
    r.readMbpsDec  = mbps;
114
12
    r.mbpsBin      = mbps / 1.048576;
115
12
    r.iops         = iops;
116
12
    r.readIops     = iops;
117
12
    r.avgLatencyMs = std::max(0.01, latBase + latJitter);
118
12
    r.maxLatencyMs = r.avgLatencyMs * (1.5 + 0.5 * std::sin(t * 0.8 + phase));
119
12
    r.cpuUtil      = 5.0 + 3.0 * idx + 2.0 * std::sin(t * 0.6 + phase);
120
12
    r.cpuUser      = r.cpuUtil * 0.4;
121
12
    r.cpuKernel    = r.cpuUtil * 0.6;
122
12
    r.errors       = 0;
123
12
    return r;
124
12
}

126
void QtDemoEngine::tick()
127
3
{
128
3
    m_t += 0.5;
129
3
    m_current.clear();
130
131
3
    for (const auto &mgr : std::as_const(m_managers)) {

        if (!mgr.connected) continue;
133
3
        for (const auto &w : mgr.workers)
134
12
            m_current.append(makeResult(w, mgr.name, m_t));   // mgr.name is std::string
135
3
    }
136
137
    // Prepend the ALL aggregate (shared core logic - same math everywhere).
138
3
    if (!m_current.isEmpty()) {
139
3
        const std::vector<WorkerResult> v(m_current.begin(), m_current.end());
140
3
        m_current.prepend(iocore::aggregateResults(v).all);
141
3
    }
142
143
3
    emit resultsUpdated(m_current);
144
3
}

353

357
0

358

359

360
0

361
0
            m_viTargets.append(dm->data.targets[i]);
362
363

364

365

366
        // None have targets pre-assigned; the user assigns them via Disk Targets tab.
367
0
        m_workers.clear();
368
0
        for (int i = 0; i < m_processorCount; ++i) {
369
0
            WorkerInfo w;
370

371
0

372
0

373
0
            w.managerName = m_managerName.toStdString();
374
0
            w.queueDepth  = 1;
375
0
            w.targets     = {};   // no targets pre-assigned
376
0
            m_workers.append(w);
377
0
        }
378
        // Tell Dynamo to create the worker threads.
379

380

0

382

384

    }

0
}

             << "workers on" << m_managerName;
400

    m_state = State::Ready;

    emit managerConnected(this);
402
0

// -- Update timer - request results while running ------------------------------

506
0
{

0

516

524

            stopTest();

550

            emit resultsReady(this);
551
0
        }
552
0

554
0
    }
555
0

{
561

565
0
    }
566
0

0

0

632
void QtDySession::startTest(const QList<WorkerInfo> &workers, const QList<AccessSpec> &specs)
633
0
{
634
0

638
0

639
640

641
0

        for (const auto &w : workers)

            if (w.managerName == m_managerName.toStdString())

647

    }

649
0

0

652
0
        for (int i = 0; i < m_processorCount; ++i) {
653
0
            WorkerInfo w;
654
0
            w.id          = QString("%1-disk-%2").arg(m_managerName).arg(i).toStdString();
655
0

656
0
            w.type        = "Disk";
657
0
            w.managerName = m_managerName.toStdString();
658
0
            w.queueDepth  = 1;
659
0
            w.targets     = {};
660
0
            m_workers.append(w);
661
0
        }
662
0
    }
663
664
0
    if (m_workers.isEmpty()) {

0
        qWarning() << "QtDySession: no targets discovered for" << m_managerName;

        return;
667
0
    }
668
669

    advanceSetupWorkers();

672
// -- Result decoding -----------------------------------------------------------
673
674
WorkerResult QtDySession::decodeWorkerResults(const DyWorkerResults &wr,
675
                                             const QString &mgrName,
676
                                             const QString &workerName)
677
0
{
678
0
    WorkerResult r;
679
0
    r.managerName = mgrName.toStdString();
680
0
    r.workerName  = workerName.toStdString();
681
0
    r.isAggregate = false;
682
683
    // Elapsed time in seconds
684
0
    const uint64_t t0 = wr.time[DY_FIRST_SNAP];
685
0
    const uint64_t t1 = wr.time[DY_LAST_SNAP];
686
0
    const double elapsed = (t1 > t0 && m_timerResolution > 0.0)
687
0
                           ? static_cast<double>(t1 - t0) / m_timerResolution
688
0
                           : 1.0;
689
690
    // Sum all targets into one counter set (counters add, max-latencies take the
691
    // max), then decode via the shared iocore formula - same math both GUIs use.
692
0
    const int tcount = std::max(0, std::min(wr.target_results.count, DY_MAX_TARGETS));
693
0
    DyRawResult summed{};
694
0
    for (int i = 0; i < tcount; ++i) {
695
0
        const DyRawResult &raw = wr.target_results.result[i];
696
0
        summed.bytes_read        += raw.bytes_read;
697
0
        summed.bytes_written     += raw.bytes_written;
698
0
        summed.read_count        += raw.read_count;
699
0
        summed.write_count       += raw.write_count;
700
0
        summed.transaction_count += raw.transaction_count;
701
0
        summed.connection_count  += raw.connection_count;
702
0
        summed.read_errors       += raw.read_errors;
703
0
        summed.write_errors      += raw.write_errors;
704
0
        summed.read_latency_sum  += raw.read_latency_sum;
705
0
        summed.write_latency_sum += raw.write_latency_sum;
706
0
        summed.max_raw_read_latency  = std::max(summed.max_raw_read_latency,  raw.max_raw_read_latency);
707
0
        summed.max_raw_write_latency = std::max(summed.max_raw_write_latency, raw.max_raw_write_latency);
708
0
    }
709
710
0
    const iocore::ResultRates d = iocore::decodeRawResult(summed, elapsed, m_timerResolution);
711
0
    r.iops         = d.iops;
712
0
    r.readIops     = d.readIops;
713
0
    r.writeIops    = d.writeIops;
714
0
    r.mbpsDec      = d.mbpsDec;
715
0
    r.readMbpsDec  = d.readMbpsDec;
716
0
    r.writeMbpsDec = d.writeMbpsDec;
717
0
    r.mbpsBin      = d.mbpsBin;
718
0
    r.errors       = static_cast<int>(d.totalErrors);
719
0
    r.avgLatencyMs = d.aveLatency;
720
0
    r.maxLatencyMs = d.maxLatency;
721
722
    // CPU from manager results (average across CPUs)
723
0
    r.cpuUtil   = 0.0;
724
0
    r.cpuUser   = 0.0;
725
0
    r.cpuKernel = 0.0;
726
0
    if (m_pendingMgrResults.cpu_results.count > 0) {
727
0
        const int ncpu = std::min(m_pendingMgrResults.cpu_results.count,
728
0
                                  DY_MAX_CPUS);
729
0
        for (int i = 0; i < ncpu; ++i) {
730
0
            r.cpuUtil   += m_pendingMgrResults.cpu_results.CPU_utilization[i][0];
731
0
            r.cpuUser   += m_pendingMgrResults.cpu_results.CPU_utilization[i][1];
732
0
            r.cpuKernel += m_pendingMgrResults.cpu_results.CPU_utilization[i][2];
733
0
        }
734
0
        r.cpuUtil   /= ncpu;
735
0
        r.cpuUser   /= ncpu;
736
0
        r.cpuKernel /= ncpu;
737
0
    }
738
739
0
    return r;
740
0
}

742
// -- Build protocol messages ---------------------------------------------------
743
744
void QtDySession::buildTestSpec(DyDataMessage *dm, const AccessSpec &spec)
745
0
{
746
0
    dm->count = 1;
747
0
    DyTestSpec &ts = dm->data.spec;
748
0
    std::strncpy(ts.name, spec.name.c_str(), sizeof(ts.name) - 1);
749
0
    ts.default_assignment = spec.defaultSpec ? 1 : 0;
750
0
    std::memset(ts.access, 0, sizeof(ts.access));
751
752
0
    const int n = qMin(static_cast<int>(spec.lines.size()), DY_MAX_ACCESS_SPECS - 1);
753
0
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
754
        // Friendly-model -> wire mapping is shared core logic (iocore).
755
0
        iocore::fillWireAccess(ts.access[i], spec.lines[i]);
756
0
    }
757
    // Terminator
758
0
    if (n < DY_MAX_ACCESS_SPECS)
759
0
        ts.access[n].of_size = 0;
760
0
}
761
762
void QtDySession::buildTargetMsg(DyDataMessage *dm, const WorkerInfo &w)
763
0
{
764
0
    dm->count = 0;
765
766
    // -- Network workers: build a TCP server/client target via the shared core
767
    //    NetworkSetup (same logic the MFC controller will use). The role comes
768
    //    from this worker's position among the session's network workers
769
    //    (single-manager loopback pairing). Disk workers fall through below.
770
0
    if (NetworkSetup::isNetworkWorker(w.type)) {
771
0
        int netCount = 0, netIndex = -1;
772
0
        for (const WorkerInfo &mw : m_workers) {
773
0
            if (NetworkSetup::isNetworkWorker(mw.type)) {
774
0
                if (mw.id == w.id) netIndex = netCount;
775
0
                ++netCount;
776
0
            }
777
0
        }
778
0
        const auto plan = NetworkSetup::planLoopback(netCount);
779
0
        if (netIndex >= 0 && netIndex < static_cast<int>(plan.size())) {
780
0
            const NetworkSetup::NetworkTarget &nt = plan[netIndex];
781
0
            DyTargetSpec &dst = dm->data.targets[0];
782
0
            std::memset(&dst, 0, sizeof(dst));
783
0
            const std::string addr = (nt.role == NetworkSetup::Role::Server)
784
0
                                     ? nt.localInterface : nt.remoteAddress;
785
0
            std::strncpy(dst.name, nt.name.c_str(), DY_MAX_NAME - 1);
786
0
            dst.type = (nt.role == NetworkSetup::Role::Server)
787
0
                       ? DY_TCP_SERVER_TYPE : DY_TCP_CLIENT_TYPE;
788
0
            std::strncpy(dst.tcp_info.remote_address, addr.c_str(), DY_MAX_NAME - 1);
789
0
            dst.queue_depth = w.queueDepth;
790
0
            dm->count = 1;
791
0
        }
792
0
        return;
793
0
    }
794
795
    // Find each target in the discovered disk list
796
0
    for (const std::string &targetName : w.targets) {
797
0
        for (const DyTargetSpec &t : m_diskTargets) {
798
0
            if (QString::fromLocal8Bit(t.name) == QString::fromStdString(targetName)) {
799
0
                if (dm->count < DY_MAX_TARGETS) {
800
0
                    DyTargetSpec &dst = dm->data.targets[dm->count];
801
0
                    std::memcpy(&dst, &t, sizeof(dst));
802
0
                    dst.queue_depth = w.queueDepth;
803
0
                    ++dm->count;
804
0
                }
805
0
                break;
806
0
            }
807
0
        }
808
0
    }
809
810
    // If no targets matched, fall back to first disk
811
0
    if (dm->count == 0 && !m_diskTargets.isEmpty()) {
812
0
        std::memcpy(&dm->data.targets[0], &m_diskTargets.first(), sizeof(DyTargetSpec));
813
0
        dm->data.targets[0].queue_depth = w.queueDepth;
814
0
        dm->count = 1;
815
0
    }
816
0
}

// QtDynamoEngine

822
QtDynamoEngine::QtDynamoEngine(bool startListening, QObject *parent)

{

23
    m_specs = QtIometerEngine::builtinAccessSpecs();

833
0

834
835
23

849
QtDynamoEngine::~QtDynamoEngine()

{

854
855
// -- New Dynamo connection -----------------------------------------------------
856
857
void QtDynamoEngine::onNewConnection()
858
0
{
859
0
    while (m_server->hasPendingConnections()) {
860
0
        QTcpSocket *sock = m_server->nextPendingConnection();
861
0
        auto *session = new QtDySession(sock, this);
862
863
0
        connect(session, &QtDySession::managerConnected,
864
0
                this, &QtDynamoEngine::onSessionConnected);
865
0
        connect(session, &QtDySession::managerDisconnected,
866
0
                this, &QtDynamoEngine::onSessionDisconnected);
867
0
        connect(session, &QtDySession::resultsReady,
868
0
                this, &QtDynamoEngine::onSessionResults);
869
0
        connect(session, &QtDySession::errorOccurred,
870
0
                this, &QtDynamoEngine::onSessionError);
871
872

// IometerTypes.h -- GUI-facing shim.
2
//
3
// The domain model (WorkerInfo, AccessSpec, ManagerInfo, WorkerResult,
4
// TestConfig, TargetInfo, ResultType, TargetKind, ...) lives in the
5
// platform-agnostic core and is shared verbatim with the MFC/Dynamo side.
6
// This header pulls those types in and adds Qt-only *display* helpers used by
7
// the GUI widgets. No Qt domain types are defined here.
8
#pragma once
9
10
#include <QString>
11
#include <QStringList>
12
#include "../core/IometerTypes.h"       // single source of truth for domain types
13
#include "../core/AccessSpecCatalog.h"  // shared access-spec naming/defaults
14
15
// ---- GUI display helpers (Qt-only; operate on the core domain types) --------
16
17
// Human-readable result-type labels, in the order used by the display menus.
18
10
inline QStringList resultTypeNames() {
19
10
    return {
20
10
        "IOps",
21
10
        "MBps (Decimal)",
22
10
        "MBps (Binary)",
23
10
        "Read IOps",
24
10
        "Write IOps",
25
10
        "Read MBps (Dec)",
26
10
        "Write MBps (Dec)",
27
10
        "Avg Latency (ms)",
28
10
        "Max Latency (ms)",
29
10
        "CPU Util (%)",
30
10
        "CPU User (%)",
31
10
        "CPU Kernel (%)",
32
10
        "Errors",
33
10
    };
34
10
}
35
36
// Format one size value as "X MiB" / "Y KiB" / "Z B" (largest unit that fits).
37
// Thin Qt wrapper over the shared core implementation.
38
17
inline QString formatSizeCompact(int bytes) {
39
17
    return QString::fromStdString(iocore::formatSize(bytes));
40
17
}
41
42
// Display label for an access spec (single-line specs show
43
// "64 KiB; 100% Read; 0% random"; others show the name). Shared core impl.
44
4
inline QString accessSpecDisplayLabel(const AccessSpec &s) {
45
4
    return QString::fromStdString(iocore::accessSpecLabel(s));
46
4
}

// Icon helpers - load directly from the original Toolbar.bmp resource strip.

// Background colour (Windows button-face grey) is made transparent.

// =============================================================================
44
45

    TBI_HELP        = 12,

448
void QtMainWindow::updateTopologyButtons()
449
55
{
450
55
    if (m_running) return;   // all topology buttons disabled while running
451
452
55
    const bool hasMgr    = !m_selManagerName.isEmpty();
453
55
    const bool hasWorker = hasMgr && !m_selWorkerId.isEmpty();
454
455
    // NewDynamo always available when not running
456
55
    m_actNewDynamo->setEnabled(true);
457
458
    // Worker creation requires a manager to be selected
459
55
    m_actNewDiskWorker->setEnabled(hasMgr);
460
55
    m_actNewNetWorker->setEnabled(hasMgr);
461
462
    // Copy only when a worker is selected
463
55
    m_actCopyWorker->setEnabled(hasWorker);
464
465
    // Exit one: a worker or manager must be selected (not "All Managers")
466
55
    m_actExitOne->setEnabled(hasMgr);
467
55
}

void QtMainWindow::onNewDiskWorker()
588
1
{
589
1
    if (m_selManagerName.isEmpty()) return;

591

1

1
    for (const auto &mgr : m_engine->managers()) {

1

            workerNum = mgr.workers.size() + 1;
596
1

597
1
        }
598
1
    }
599
1
    WorkerInfo w;

1
    w.id          = QString("%1-disk-%2").arg(m_selManagerName).arg(workerNum).toStdString();

    w.name        = QString("Worker %1").arg(workerNum).toStdString();
602

    w.type        = "Disk";
603
1
    w.managerName = m_selManagerName.toStdString();
604
1

605
1

1

void QtMainWindow::onCopyWorker()

{

1

1

1
            w.name        = src.name + " (copy)";

            m_engine->addWorker(m_selManagerName, w);

// QtMeterWidget.cpp
2
// Qt port of CMeterCtrl - Iometer speedometer gauge.
3
//
4
// Geometry notes
5
// --------------
6
// The sweep runs 270° from lower-left (value=0) clockwise through 12-o'clock
7
// (value=max/2) to lower-right (value=max).  The 90° "notch" at the bottom
8
// is where the scale text lives.
9
//
10
// CalculatePoint(θ, r):   real_angle = θ + 90 + PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE  (= θ + 135)
11
//   x = pivot.x + cos(real_angle°) · r
12
//   y = pivot.y + sin(real_angle°) · r     (y increases downward, same as MFC)
13
//
14
// Qt arc angles: 0° = east, positive = CCW, 1/16° units.
15
// Meter θ → Qt degrees: -(θ + 90 + PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE)
16
// CW sweep = negative spanAngle in Qt.
17
18
#include "QtMeterWidget.h"
19
#include <QPainter>
20
#include <QPainterPath>
21
#include <QResizeEvent>
22
#include <cmath>
23
24
static constexpr double PI = 3.14159265358979323846;

26
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
27

28
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

30

49
{
33
49
    setAttribute(Qt::WA_OpaquePaintEvent);   // we fill the entire widget
34
49
    setMinimumSize(120, 90);
35
49

QPointF QtMeterWidget::calcPt(int angle, int radius) const
47
1.23k
{
48
    // Matches CMeterCtrl::CalculatePoint exactly.
49
1.23k
    const double a = (angle + 90 + PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE) * PI / 180.0;
50
1.23k
    return { m_pivot.x() + std::cos(a) * radius,
51
1.23k
             m_pivot.y() + std::sin(a) * radius };

1.23k

void QtMeterWidget::recalcGeometry()
55

{
56
17
    const int w = width();
57
17
    const int h = height();
58
17
    if (w < 20 || h < 20) return;
59
60
    // Largest outer_radius that keeps the whole visible arc inside the widget.
61
    // The arc bottoms out at y = outer_radius * (1 + cos(PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE)).
62
17
    const double cosPA = std::cos(PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE * PI / 180.0);  // cos(45°) ≈ 0.7071
63
17
    m_outerRadius  = static_cast<int>(std::min(w / 2.0, h / (1.0 + cosPA)));
64
17
    m_innerRadius  = static_cast<int>(m_outerRadius * 0.70);
65
17
    m_pivotRadius  = std::max(3, m_innerRadius / 12);

67
17

68
17
    m_labelBoxSize = std::max(6, static_cast<int>(m_outerRadius * 0.10));
69
70
    // Pivot at horizontal centre, one outer_radius down from the top.
71
17
    m_pivot = { w / 2.0, static_cast<double>(m_outerRadius) };

73
17
    updateScaleInfo();
74
17
    updateLabelInfo();

    setNeedlePoints();

// QtMeterWidget.h
2
// Qt port of CMeterCtrl - the Iometer speedometer gauge control.
3
//
4
// Drop-in replacement geometry: same NEEDLE_SWEEP, PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE, and
5
// CalculatePoint() math as the original MFC control, rewritten with QPainter.
6
//
7
// Usage:
8
//   QtMeterWidget *m = new QtMeterWidget(parent);
9
//   m->setRange(0, 100, /*autoRange=*/true);
10
//   m->setValue(42.0);
11
//   m->showWatermark = true;
12
#pragma once
13
14
#include <QWidget>
15
#include <QPointF>
16
#include <QRectF>
17
#include <QString>
18
#include <vector>
19
20
class QtMeterWidget : public QWidget
21
{
22
    Q_OBJECT
23
24
public:
25
    explicit QtMeterWidget(QWidget *parent = nullptr);
26
27
    // -- API matching CMeterCtrl -------------------------------------------
28
    void setRange(int range1, int range2, bool autoRange = false);
29
    void setValue(double newValue);
30
    void resetWatermark();
31
32
    bool showWatermark = false;   // public, mirrors CMeterCtrl::show_watermark
33
34
    QSize sizeHint() const override;
35
36
protected:
37
    void paintEvent(QPaintEvent *event) override;
38
    void resizeEvent(QResizeEvent *event) override;
39
40
private:
41
    // -- Constants (match MeterCtrl.h) ------------------------------------
42
    static constexpr int NEEDLE_SWEEP       = 270;                    // degrees
43
    static constexpr int PIVOT_ARC_ANGLE    = (360 - NEEDLE_SWEEP) / 2; // 45°
44
    static constexpr int NEEDLE_SENSITIVITY = 3;                      // degrees
45
46
    // -- Range / value ----------------------------------------------------
47
    int    m_minRange  = 0;
48
    int    m_maxRange  = 100;
49
    bool   m_autoRange = true;
50
    double m_value     = 0.0;
51
52
    // -- Watermark --------------------------------------------------------
53
    double m_lowValue  = -1.0;   // < 0 means "not set yet"
54
    double m_highValue =  0.0;
55
56
    // -- Needle angles (meter-space: 0=min, NEEDLE_SWEEP=max) -------------
57
    int m_actualAngle = 0;
58
    int m_shownAngle  = 0;
59
60
    // -- Geometry (recalculated on resize) ---------------------------------
61
    QPointF m_pivot;
62
    int     m_outerRadius  = 0;
63
    int     m_innerRadius  = 0;
64
    int     m_pivotRadius  = 0;
65
    int     m_labelRadius  = 0;
66
    int     m_tickRadius   = 0;
67
    int     m_labelBoxSize = 0;
68
69
    // -- Needle triangle (3 vertices) -------------------------------------
70
    QPointF m_needle[3];
71
72
    // -- Label / tick-mark cache -------------------------------------------
73
    struct LabelInfo {
74
        QRectF  box;
75
        QString text;
76
        QPointF tickInner;
77
        QPointF tickOuter;
78
    };
79
    std::vector<LabelInfo> m_labels;
80
    QString m_scaleText;          // e.g. "x1,000"
81
82
    // -- Private helpers ---------------------------------------------------
83
    void recalcGeometry();
84
    void updateScaleInfo();
85
    void updateLabelInfo();
86
    void setNeedlePoints();
87
88
    // Convert a meter-space angle (0…NEEDLE_SWEEP) to a screen point.
89
    // Exact translation of CMeterCtrl::CalculatePoint.
90
    QPointF calcPt(int angle, int radius) const;
91
};

// =============================================================================
267
// Spec editor dialog
268
// =============================================================================
269
270
// -- Helper --------------------------------------------------------------------
271
// Build three spinners (Megabytes / Kilobytes / Bytes) packed into an HBox.
272
// *mbSpin, *kbSpin, *bSpin are set to the created spinners.
273
static QHBoxLayout *makeSizeSpinners(QSpinBox **mbSpin, QSpinBox **kbSpin,
274
                                     QSpinBox **bSpin)
275
9
{
276
9
    *mbSpin = new QSpinBox; (*mbSpin)->setRange(0, 8192); (*mbSpin)->setSuffix("");
277
9
    *kbSpin = new QSpinBox; (*kbSpin)->setRange(0, 65535);
278
9
    *bSpin  = new QSpinBox; (*bSpin)->setRange(0, 65535);
279
9
    auto *lay = new QHBoxLayout;
280
9
    lay->setSpacing(4);
281
9
    lay->addWidget(*mbSpin); lay->addWidget(new QLabel("Megabytes"));
282
9
    lay->addWidget(*kbSpin); lay->addWidget(new QLabel("Kilobytes"));
283
9
    lay->addWidget(*bSpin);  lay->addWidget(new QLabel("Bytes"));
284
9
    return lay;
285
9
}
286
static void setSizeSpinners(QSpinBox *mb, QSpinBox *kb, QSpinBox *b, int bytes)
287
8
{
288
8
    mb->setValue(bytes / 1048576);
289
8
    kb->setValue((bytes % 1048576) / 1024);
290
8
    b ->setValue(bytes % 1024);
291
8
}
292
static int readSizeSpinners(QSpinBox *mb, QSpinBox *kb, QSpinBox *b)
293
3
{
294
3
    return mb->value() * 1048576 + kb->value() * 1024 + b->value();
295
3
}
296

644
645
// Load the engine's spec list into a transient core library so the list
646
// operations (new/copy naming, removal) use the shared MFC-canonical semantics.
647
static iocore::AccessSpecLibrary libraryFromEngine(QtIometerEngine *engine)
648
3
{
649
3
    iocore::AccessSpecLibrary lib;
650
3
    std::vector<AccessSpec> v;
651
3
    const auto specs = engine->accessSpecs();
652
3
    v.reserve(specs.size());
653
96
    for (const auto &s : specs) v.push_back(s);
654
3
    lib.setSpecs(std::move(v));
655
3
    return lib;
656
3
}
657
658
static void libraryToEngine(const iocore::AccessSpecLibrary &lib, QtIometerEngine *engine)
659
3
{
660
3
    QList<AccessSpec> out;
661
97
    for (const auto &s : lib.specs()) out.append(s);
662
3
    engine->setAccessSpecs(out);
663
3
}
664
665
void QtPageAccess::onNewSpec()
666
1
{
667
    // Core semantics: "Untitled n" + the default line (2 KiB, 67% read, random).
668
1
    iocore::AccessSpecLibrary lib = libraryFromEngine(m_engine);
669
1
    const int idx = lib.newSpec();
670
1
    AccessSpec s = lib.at(idx);
671
1
    if (!editSpecDialog(s, "New Access Specification")) return;
672
1
    lib.at(idx) = s;
673
1
    libraryToEngine(lib, m_engine);
674
1
    loadSpecList();
675
1
    m_global->setCurrentRow(m_global->count() - 1);
676
1
}
677
678
void QtPageAccess::onEditSpec()
679
1
{
680
1
    const int row = m_global->currentRow();
681
1
    if (row < 0) return;
682
1
    auto specs = m_engine->accessSpecs();
683
1
    if (row >= specs.size()) return;
684
1
    AccessSpec s = specs[row];
685
1
    if (!editSpecDialog(s, "Edit Access Specification")) return;
686
0
    specs[row] = s;
687
0
    m_engine->setAccessSpecs(specs);
688
0
    loadSpecList();
689
0
    m_global->setCurrentRow(row);
690
0
}

692
void QtPageAccess::onEditCopySpec()
693
1
{
694
1
    const int row = m_global->currentRow();
695
1
    if (row < 0) return;
696
    // Core semantics: unique "Copy of <name> (n)" (appended at the end, as the
697
    // original Iometer does).
698
1
    iocore::AccessSpecLibrary lib = libraryFromEngine(m_engine);
699
1
    if (row >= lib.count()) return;
700
1
    const int idx = lib.copySpec(row);
701
1
    if (idx < 0) return;
702
1
    AccessSpec s = lib.at(idx);
703
1
    if (!editSpecDialog(s, "Edit Copy of Access Specification")) return;
704
1
    lib.at(idx) = s;
705
1
    libraryToEngine(lib, m_engine);
706
1
    loadSpecList();
707
1
    m_global->setCurrentRow(m_global->count() - 1);
708
1
}
709
710
void QtPageAccess::onDeleteSpec()
711
1
{
712
1
    const int row = m_global->currentRow();
713
1
    if (row < 0) return;
714
1
    iocore::AccessSpecLibrary lib = libraryFromEngine(m_engine);
715
1
    if (row >= lib.count()) return;
716
1
    const auto btn = QMessageBox::question(this, "Delete Spec",
717
1
        QString("Delete \"%1\"?").arg(QString::fromStdString(lib.at(row).name)));
718
1
    if (btn != QMessageBox::Yes) return;
719
1
    lib.removeAt(row);
720
1
    libraryToEngine(lib, m_engine);
721
1
    loadSpecList();
722
1
}

// QtPageAccess.h -- "Access Specifications" tab.
2
// Layout matches the original:
3
//   Left:   "Assigned Access Specifications" list + Move Up/Down
4
//   Center: << Add / Remove >> transfer buttons
5
//   Right:  "Global Access Specifications" list + New/Edit/Edit Copy/Delete
6
#pragma once
7
#include "QtIometerTypes.h"
8
#include <QWidget>
9
10
class QtIometerEngine;
11
class QListWidget;
12
class QPushButton;
13
class QDialog;
14
15
class QtPageAccess : public QWidget
16
{
17
    Q_OBJECT
18
public:
19
    explicit QtPageAccess(QtIometerEngine *engine, QWidget *parent = nullptr);
20
21
public slots:
22
    void loadSpecList();   // rebuild both panels from engine state
23
    void setActiveSpecIndex(int idx);  // highlight the currently-running assigned spec (-1 = none)
24
25
    // Returns the AccessSpec objects currently in the Assigned list, in order.
26
    QList<AccessSpec> currentAssignedSpecs() const;
27
28
private slots:
29
    void onAddToAssigned();
30
    void onRemoveFromAssigned();
31
    void onMoveUp();
32
    void onMoveDown();
33
    void onNewSpec();
34
    void onEditSpec();
35
    void onEditCopySpec();
36
    void onDeleteSpec();
37
    void onGlobalSelectionChanged();
38
    void onAssignedSelectionChanged();
39
40
private:
41
    void setupUi();
42
    void rebuildGlobalList();
43
    void rebuildAssignedList();
44
    bool editSpecDialog(AccessSpec &spec, const QString &title);
45
46
    QtIometerEngine *m_engine    = nullptr;
47
    QListWidget   *m_assigned  = nullptr;   // left column
48
    QListWidget   *m_global    = nullptr;   // right column
49
    QPushButton   *m_addBtn    = nullptr;   // << Add
50
    QPushButton   *m_removeBtn = nullptr;   // Remove >>
51
    QPushButton   *m_upBtn     = nullptr;
52
    QPushButton   *m_downBtn   = nullptr;
53
    QPushButton   *m_newBtn    = nullptr;
54
    QPushButton   *m_editBtn   = nullptr;
55
    QPushButton   *m_copyBtn   = nullptr;
56
    QPushButton   *m_delBtn    = nullptr;
57
};

// Helpers

// =============================================================================
191
192
// Smallest power of 10 strictly greater than val.
193
// Examples:  0.0329 → 0.1,  30366 → 100000,  995 → 1000,  3.89 → 10
194
static double logCeiling(double val)
195
24
{
196
24
    if (val <= 0.0) return 1.0;
197
24
    return std::pow(10.0, std::floor(std::log10(val)) + 1.0);
198
24
}
199
200
// Format the scale ceiling for display
201
static QString scaleText(double ceiling)
202
30
{
203
30
    if (ceiling >= 1.0)   return QString::number(static_cast<qint64>(ceiling));
204
0
    if (ceiling >= 0.01)  return QString::number(ceiling, 'f', 1);   // "0.1"
205
0
    return                       QString::number(ceiling, 'f', 2);   // "0.01"
206
0
}

// Layout matches the original: metric rows showing live values + BigMeter access.
3

4
5

{
21
    Q_OBJECT
22
23
public:
24
    explicit QtPageDisplay(QtIometerEngine *engine, QWidget *parent = nullptr);
25
26
public slots:
27
    void updateResults(const QVector<WorkerResult> &results);
28
    void onTestStarted();
29
    void onTestStopped();
30
    void onBigMeterClicked();
31
    void refreshWorkerAssignments();

33
signals:

38

39
40

41
    // One display row (matches original "Total I/Os per Second" row etc.)
42
    struct DisplayRow {
43
        int          resultType;    // RESULT_* constant
44
        QPushButton *metricBtn;     // left button with metric name
45
        QLabel      *workerLbl;     // "All Managers"
46
        QLabel      *valueLbl;      // live numeric value (separate from the bar)
47
        QProgressBar*bar;           // proportional fill indicator (no text)
48
        QLabel      *refLbl;        // scale ceiling (power of 10)
49
        QPushButton *expandBtn;     // ">" -- opens BigMeter
50
        double       refValue = 0.0;
51
        double       maxSeen  = 0.0; // 0 = not yet set; updated to pow-of-10 ceiling
52
    };
53
54
    void setupUi();
55
    void setupRow(QWidget *container, int row, int resultType, const QString &label);
56
57
    QtIometerEngine  *m_engine     = nullptr;
58
    QtBigMeterWidget *m_bigMeter   = nullptr;
59
    QCheckBox      *m_recordChk  = nullptr;
60
    QRadioButton   *m_sinceStart = nullptr;
61
    QRadioButton   *m_sinceUpdate= nullptr;
62
    QComboBox      *m_freqCombo  = nullptr;
63
64

65
    DisplayRow  m_rows[NUM_ROWS];
66
    int         m_activeMeterRow = 0;
67
    bool        m_running        = false;
68
};

1
// QtPageNetwork.h -- "Network Targets" tab.
2
// Layout matches the original:
3
//   Left:   "Targets" group box with a checkable manager->NIC tree
4
//   Right:  Network Interface to Use for Connection,
5
//           Max # Outstanding Sends, Test Connection Rate
6
#pragma once
7
#include "QtIometerTypes.h"
8
#include <QWidget>
9
10
class QtIometerEngine;
11
class QTreeWidget;
12
class QTreeWidgetItem;
13
class QComboBox;
14
class QSpinBox;
15
class QCheckBox;
16
class QGroupBox;
17
18
class QtPageNetwork : public QWidget
19
{
20
    Q_OBJECT
21
public:
22
    explicit QtPageNetwork(QtIometerEngine *engine, QWidget *parent = nullptr);
23
24
public slots:
25
    // Called by QtMainWindow when Topology tree selection changes
26
    void clearSelection();
27
    void setSelectedManager(const QString &mgrName);
28
    void setSelectedWorker(const QString &mgrName, const QString &workerId);
29
30
    // Called when engine reports new config (e.g. manager connected)
31
    void refreshForEngine();
32
33
    // Called by QtMainWindow for compatibility
34
    void onManagerConnected(const ManagerInfo &mgr);
35
    void onManagerDisconnected(const QString &name);
36
37
private slots:
38
    void onTargetItemChanged(QTreeWidgetItem *item, int column);
39
    void onNicComboChanged(int index);
40
    void onMaxSendsChanged(int value);
41
    void onTestConnRateToggled(bool checked);
42
    void onTransPerConnChanged(int value);
43
44
private:
45
    void setupUi();
46
    void populateTargetTree();
47
    void loadWorkerParams();
48
    void saveWorkerParams();
49
    void setParamsEnabled(bool enabled);
50
    void rebuildNicCombo();
51
52
    QtIometerEngine   *m_engine          = nullptr;
53
    QString          m_selManagerName;
54
    QString          m_selWorkerId;
55
    bool             m_updating        = false;
56
    // Set while this page is writing its own edit back to the engine, so the
57
    // engine's resulting configChanged doesn't re-enter refreshForEngine and
58
    // rebuild the tree/combo out from under the widget signal in flight.
59
    bool             m_applyingEdit    = false;
60
61
    // Left panel
62
    QTreeWidget     *m_targetTree      = nullptr;
63
64
    // Right panel
65
    QComboBox       *m_nicCombo        = nullptr;
66
    QSpinBox        *m_maxSends        = nullptr;
67
    QCheckBox       *m_testConnRateChk = nullptr;
68
    QSpinBox        *m_transPerConn    = nullptr;
69
};

172
void QtPageResults::loadConfig()
173
27
{
174
27
    m_updating = true;
175
27
    const TestConfig &c = m_engine->testConfig();
176
27
    m_description->setText(QString::fromStdString(c.description));
177
27
    m_runHours->setValue(c.runHours);
178
27
    m_runMinutes->setValue(c.runMinutes);
179
27
    m_runSeconds->setValue(c.runSeconds);
180
27
    m_rampSeconds->setValue(c.rampSeconds);
181
27
    m_recordResults->setCurrentIndex(c.recordResults);
182
27
    m_cyclingMode->setCurrentIndex(c.cyclingMode);
183
27
    m_workerStart->setValue(c.workerStart);
184
27
    m_workerStep->setValue(c.workerStep);
185
27
    m_workerStepping->setCurrentIndex(c.workerStepping);
186
27
    m_targetStart->setValue(c.targetStart);
187
27
    m_targetStep->setValue(c.targetStep);
188
27
    m_targetStepping->setCurrentIndex(c.targetStepping);
189
27
    m_ioqStart->setValue(c.ioqStart);
190
27
    m_ioqEnd->setValue(c.ioqEnd);
191
27
    m_ioqPower->setValue(c.ioqPower);
192
27
    m_ioqStepping->setCurrentIndex(c.ioqStepping);
193
27
    m_updating = false;
194
27
}

203
2

2

2

208

209
2

213

215
2
    c.ioqPower      = m_ioqPower->value();

// Build the "unprepared logical disk" icon: take a standard HD icon and
26

27
static QIcon makeUnpreparedDiskIcon()
28

{
29
2
    const int sz = 16;

2

2

34

35
0

36
0
    }
37
2

38
2

39

2

    p.end();
43

1
// QtPageSetup.h -- "Disk Targets" tab.
2
// Equivalent to CPageSetup / CDiskPageDlg in the MFC original.
3
//
4
// Layout matches the original:
5
//   Left:   "Targets" group box with a checkable target list
6
//   Right:  Maximum Disk Size, Starting Disk Sector, # Outstanding I/Os,
7
//           Use Fixed Seed, Test Connection Rate, Write IO Data Pattern
8
#pragma once
9
#include "QtIometerTypes.h"
10
#include <QWidget>
11
12
class QtIometerEngine;
13
class QListWidget;
14
class QListWidgetItem;
15
class QSpinBox;
16
class QCheckBox;
17
class QComboBox;
18
class QLabel;
19
class QGroupBox;
20
class QLineEdit;
21
22
class QtPageSetup : public QWidget
23
{
24
    Q_OBJECT
25
public:
26
    explicit QtPageSetup(QtIometerEngine *engine, QWidget *parent = nullptr);
27
28
public slots:
29
    // Called by QtMainWindow when the Topology tree selection changes
30
    void clearSelection();
31
    void setSelectedManager(const QString &mgrName);
32
    void setSelectedWorker(const QString &mgrName, const QString &workerId);
33
34
    // Called when the engine reports new config (e.g. manager connected)
35
    void refreshForEngine();
36
37
private slots:
38
    void onTargetItemChanged(QListWidgetItem *item);
39
    void onQueueDepthChanged(int value);
40
    void onMaxDiskSizeChanged(int value);
41
    void onStartSectorChanged(int value);
42
    void onFixedSeedToggled(bool checked);
43
    void onFixedSeedValueChanged();
44
    void onConnRateToggled(bool checked);
45
    void onTransPerConnChanged(int value);
46
    void onDataPatternChanged(int index);
47
48
private:
49
    void setupUi();
50
    void populateTargetList();
51
    void loadWorkerParams();
52
    void saveWorkerParams();
53
    void setParamsEnabled(bool enabled);
54
55
    QtIometerEngine  *m_engine         = nullptr;
56
    QString         m_selManagerName;
57
    QString         m_selWorkerId;
58
    bool            m_updating        = false;
59
    // Set while writing our own edit back to the engine, so the resulting
60
    // configChanged doesn't re-enter refreshForEngine and rebuild the target list
61
    // out from under the itemChanged signal in flight (use-after-free).
62
    bool            m_applyingEdit    = false;
63
64
    // Left panel
65
    QListWidget    *m_targetList      = nullptr;
66
67
    // Right panel
68
    QSpinBox       *m_maxDiskSize     = nullptr;   // sectors
69
    QSpinBox       *m_startSector     = nullptr;   // sectors
70
    QSpinBox       *m_queueDepth      = nullptr;   // per target
71
    QCheckBox      *m_fixedSeedChk    = nullptr;
72
    QLineEdit      *m_fixedSeedEdit   = nullptr;
73
    QCheckBox      *m_connRateChk     = nullptr;
74
    QSpinBox       *m_transPerConn    = nullptr;
75
    QComboBox      *m_dataPattern     = nullptr;
76
};
